paper_metadata.title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts
paper_metadata.abstract: Scaling language models with more data, compute and parameters has driven significant progress in natural language processing. For example, thanks to scaling, GPT-3 was able to achieve strong results on in-context learning tasks. However, training these large dense models requires significant amounts of computing resources. In this paper, we propose and develop a family of language models named GLaM (Generalist Language Model), which uses a sparsely activated mixture-of-experts architecture to scale the model capacity while also incurring substantially less training cost compared to dense variants. The largest GLaM has 1.2 trillion parameters, which is approximately 7x larger than GPT-3. It consumes only 1/3 of the energy used to train GPT-3 and requires half of the computation flops for inference, while still achieving better overall zero-shot and one-shot performance across 29 NLP tasks.

extraction_data.innovation: GLaM uses a mixture-of-experts architecture to sparsely activate a subset of parameters (64B out of 1.2T), enabling efficient scaling while maintaining performance on few-shot learning tasks.
extraction_data.extension: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture for sparse parameter activation.
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient scaling of large language models while maintaining performance and reducing computational resources.
extraction_data.application: General language understanding, few-shot learning, natural language processing tasks
extraction_data.paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

extraction_data.innovation: GLaM uses a dense decoder-only architecture with Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) layers to efficiently scale language models while maintaining computational efficiency. The model dynamically selects the best two experts per token, reducing computational cost.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Filtered Webpages, Wikipedia, Conversations, Books, News, Forums
extraction_data.hardware_used: NVIDIA V100 GPUs (as inferred from similar works)
extraction_data.extension: The use of Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) layers allows for sparse activation of experts, reducing computational cost while maintaining model capacity.
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient scaling of large language models while maintaining performance on few-shot learning tasks.
extraction_data.application: General language understanding, few-shot learning, natural language processing tasks
extraction_data.paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

extraction_data.innovation: GLaM introduces a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture where each Transformer layer is replaced by a sparse MoE layer, enabling efficient scaling of language models while maintaining computational efficiency. The model dynamically selects the best two experts per token, reducing computational cost.

extraction_data.innovation: GLaM introduces a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture that sparsely activates a subset of parameters per prediction, enabling efficient scaling of language models while maintaining performance. This approach reduces computational and energy costs compared to dense models.
